methodsAttempted.query: site:ucsd.edu UC San Diego generative AI policy students teaching artificial intelligence academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the UC San Diego Academic Integrity Office AI landing page, Skaggs School pharmacy guidelines, a Senate workgroup report PDF, the student ChatGPT guidance page, UC San Diego Today stories, and the Academic Senate integrity policy.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ucsd.edu "generative AI" "UC San Diego" "teaching" "guidance" OR "privacy" OR "academic integrity"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search confirmed the Academic Integrity Office AI page, the Senate workgroup education report, UC San Diego Today stories, an AI convening event page, and the 2025 research workgroup report PDF.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ucsd.edu "AI" "privacy" "UC San Diego" "Copilot" OR "ChatGPT" official guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Search results were mostly generic, course, research, or event pages, so direct official-source expansion was used for Blink AI services and Library GenAI guide pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ucsd.libguides.com "Generative AI" "UC San Diego" students guide use citation
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the UC San Diego Library Generative AI guide and the AI and Academic Integrity subpage.
The Library guide linked the official Blink AI services page, which was fetched directly as a verified source for supported services and security-review claims.

artifacts.summary: Firecrawl search and direct official-source expansion found UC San Diego Senate, Academic Integrity Office, Library, Blink, and Senate-Administration workgroup sources that directly address AI use, academic integrity, supported AI services, data security, and research guidance. Pharmacy-specific, news-only, event/speaker, and SharePoint-linked sources were rejected or marked inaccessible for this university-wide staged run.

artifacts.claimText: UC San Diego's Academic Integrity Policy says students may not let academic work or academic credit be completed for them by another human or by machine/artificial intelligence, and may not use unauthorized aids including artificial intelligence in coursework or assessments.

citation.citationTitle: UC San Diego Academic Integrity Policy
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UC San Diego Academic Integrity Office student guidance says that if an instructor has not said a student can use GenAI for a class or assessment, the student cannot use it; silence does not equal permission.

citation.publisher: UC San Diego Academic Integrity Office
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UC San Diego Academic Integrity Office guidance says students authorized to use a GenAI tool should use it only in the way authorized for that assignment, should not assume authorization extends to other assignments or courses, and are advised to save history and acknowledge use to the professor.

artifacts.sourceTitle: UC San Diego Academic Integrity Policy
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: No student shall allow any academic work or academic credit to be completed or obtained, in part or in whole, for themselves by another (human or machine/artificial intelligence). No student shall employ aids (including artificial intelligence) in undertaking course work or in completing any assessments that are not authorized by the instructor.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using ChatGPT
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Yes, you're good to use the GenAI tool in the way that instructor authorized for that particular assignment. Don't assume you can use it in ways not authorized or for other assignments or courses. Also, if you use it, save your history so you can share it and acknowledge your use to the professor.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: So, if the instructor said I could use it, I'm good to go?

artifacts.sourceTitle: UC San Diego's Support of AI
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: TritonGPT, our preferred Generative AI platform, is securely hosted at the San Diego Supercomputer Center. By leveraging a combination of open-source large language models (LLMs) and secure on-premise hosting, TritonGPT ensures that UC San Diego maintains full control over our data.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines for the Use of Generative AI in Research at UC San Diego
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: We recommend that UC San Diego’s responsible approach to GenAI in research be grounded in five core guidelines that build on the eight UC Responsible AI Principles: Build from a foundation of research integrity; Ensure human oversight; Respect evolving disciplinary norms; Foster responsible innovation; Prioritize principles over prescriptions.

artifacts.notes: Approved as narrow academic-integrity claim directly supported by official Academic Senate policy text.
Approved because the claim mirrors the official student-facing permission language and avoids broader policy conclusions.
Approved as scoped to authorized use, assignment limits, and acknowledgement advice on the AIO page.
Approved as a teaching-resource claim about the AIO Canvas module, not a mandate.
Approved with careful 'might be cheating' wording matching the Library guide.
Approved as direct supported-services claim from the Blink AI services page.
Approved as a security-review claim directly supported by Blink's evaluation criteria language.
Approved with wording scoped to a workgroup report's proposed guidance, not a final binding policy.
Approved because the claim preserves the report's data-handling scope and avoids turning should/may language into a mandate.

artifacts.title: University of California, San Diego staged AI policy crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source crawl for University of California, San Diego found campus-wide Academic Senate, Academic Integrity Office, Library, Blink, and Senate-Administration Workgroup evidence. Nine candidate claims were agent-reviewed from official evidence. School-specific, news-only, event-only, and SharePoint-linked sources were rejected or excluded. No canonical publication or human review was asserted.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The 2025 research PDF was staged as proposed workgroup guidance and recommendations, not as a binding final research policy.
The pharmacy-specific page was rejected for this university-wide run and may require a separate school-level record if that scope is later supported.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
